speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Fair Queen of England, worthy Margaret,

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Sit down with us. It ill befits thy state

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: And birth that thou shouldst stand while Lewis doth sit.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: No, mighty King of France. Now Margaret

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Must strike her sail and learn awhile to serve

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Where kings command. I was, I must confess,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Great Albion’s queen in former golden days,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But now mischance hath trod my title down

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And with dishonor laid me on the ground,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Where I must take like seat unto my fortune

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And to my humble seat conform myself.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Why, say, fair queen, whence springs this deep despair?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: From such a cause as fills mine eyes with tears

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And stops my tongue, while heart is drowned in cares.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Whate’er it be, be thou still like thyself,

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: And sit thee by our side.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Yield not thy neck

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: To Fortune’s yoke, but let thy dauntless mind

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Still ride in triumph over all mischance.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Be plain, Queen Margaret, and tell thy grief.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: It shall be eased if France can yield relief.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Those gracious words revive my drooping thoughts

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And give my tongue-tied sorrows leave to speak.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Now therefore be it known to noble Lewis

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That Henry, sole possessor of my love,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Is, of a king, become a banished man

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And forced to live in Scotland a forlorn;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: While proud ambitious Edward, Duke of York,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Usurps the regal title and the seat

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Of England’s true-anointed lawful king.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: This is the cause that I, poor Margaret,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: With this my son, Prince Edward, Henry’s heir,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Am come to crave thy just and lawful aid;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And if thou fail us, all our hope is done.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Scotland hath will to help but cannot help;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Our people and our peers are both misled,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Our treasure seized, our soldiers put to flight,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And, as thou seest, ourselves in heavy plight.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Renownèd queen, with patience calm the storm

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: While we bethink a means to break it off.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The more we stay, the stronger grows our foe.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: The more I stay, the more I’ll succor thee.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: O, but impatience waiteth on true sorrow.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And see where comes the breeder of my sorrow.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: What’s he approacheth boldly to our presence?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Our Earl of Warwick, Edward’s greatest friend.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Welcome, brave Warwick. What brings thee to France?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Ay, now begins a second storm to rise,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For this is he that moves both wind and tide.

speaker: WARWICK
text: From worthy Edward, King of Albion,

speaker: WARWICK
text: My lord and sovereign and thy vowèd friend,

speaker: WARWICK
text: I come in kindness and unfeignèd love,

speaker: WARWICK
text: First, to do greetings to thy royal person,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And then to crave a league of amity,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And, lastly, to confirm that amity

speaker: WARWICK
text: With nuptial knot, if thou vouchsafe to grant

speaker: WARWICK
text: That virtuous Lady Bona, thy fair sister,

speaker: WARWICK
text: To England’s king in lawful marriage.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: If that go forward, Henry’s hope is done.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And, gracious madam, in our king’s behalf,

speaker: WARWICK
text: I am commanded, with your leave and favor,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Humbly to kiss your hand, and with my tongue

speaker: WARWICK
text: To tell the passion of my sovereign’s heart,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Where fame, late ent’ring at his heedful ears,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Hath placed thy beauty’s image and thy virtue.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: King Lewis and Lady Bona, hear me speak

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Before you answer Warwick. His demand

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Springs not from Edward’s well-meant honest love,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But from deceit, bred by necessity;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For how can tyrants safely govern home

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Unless abroad they purchase great alliance?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: To prove him tyrant, this reason may suffice:

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That Henry liveth still; but were he dead,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Yet here Prince Edward stands, King Henry’s son.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Look, therefore, Lewis, that by this league and marriage

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thou draw not on thy danger and dishonor;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For though usurpers sway the rule awhile,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Yet heav’ns are just, and time suppresseth wrongs.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Injurious Margaret!

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: And why not “Queen”?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Because thy father Henry did usurp,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And thou no more art prince than she is queen.

speaker: OXFORD
text: Then Warwick disannuls great John of Gaunt,

speaker: OXFORD
text: Which did subdue the greatest part of Spain;

speaker: OXFORD
text: And after John of Gaunt, Henry the Fourth,

speaker: OXFORD
text: Whose wisdom was a mirror to the wisest;

speaker: OXFORD
text: And after that wise prince, Henry the Fifth,

speaker: OXFORD
text: Who by his prowess conquerèd all France.

speaker: OXFORD
text: From these our Henry lineally descends.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Oxford, how haps it in this smooth discourse

speaker: WARWICK
text: You told not how Henry the Sixth hath lost

speaker: WARWICK
text: All that which Henry the Fifth had gotten.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Methinks these peers of France should smile at that.

speaker: WARWICK
text: But, for the rest: you tell a pedigree

speaker: WARWICK
text: Of threescore and two years, a silly time

speaker: WARWICK
text: To make prescription for a kingdom’s worth.

speaker: OXFORD
text: Why, Warwick, canst thou speak against thy liege,

speaker: OXFORD
text: Whom thou obeyed’st thirty and six years,

speaker: OXFORD
text: And not bewray thy treason with a blush?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Can Oxford, that did ever fence the right,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Now buckler falsehood with a pedigree?

speaker: WARWICK
text: For shame, leave Henry, and call Edward king.

speaker: OXFORD
text: Call him my king, by whose injurious doom

speaker: OXFORD
text: My elder brother, the Lord Aubrey Vere,

speaker: OXFORD
text: Was done to death? And more than so, my father,

speaker: OXFORD
text: Even in the downfall of his mellowed years,

speaker: OXFORD
text: When nature brought him to the door of death?

speaker: OXFORD
text: No, Warwick, no. While life upholds this arm,

speaker: OXFORD
text: This arm upholds the house of Lancaster.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And I the house of York.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Queen Margaret, Prince Edward, and Oxford,

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Vouchsafe, at our request, to stand aside

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: While I use further conference with Warwick.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Heavens grant that Warwick’s words bewitch him not.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Now, Warwick, tell me, even upon thy conscience,

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Is Edward your true king? For I were loath

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: To link with him that were not lawful chosen.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Thereon I pawn my credit and mine honor.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: But is he gracious in the people’s eye?

speaker: WARWICK
text: The more that Henry was unfortunate.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Then further, all dissembling set aside,

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Tell me for truth the measure of his love

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Unto our sister Bona.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Such it seems

speaker: WARWICK
text: As may beseem a monarch like himself.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Myself have often heard him say and swear

speaker: WARWICK
text: That this his love was an eternal plant,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Whereof the root was fixed in virtue’s ground,

speaker: WARWICK
text: The leaves and fruit maintained with beauty’s sun,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Exempt from envy but not from disdain,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Unless the Lady Bona quit his pain.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Now, sister, let us hear your firm resolve.

speaker:
LADY
               
              BONA
text: Your grant or your denial shall be mine.

speaker:
LADY
               
              BONA
text:
(
                Speaks
                 
                to
                 
                Warwick
                .
                )
                 
              Yet I confess that often ere this day,

speaker:
LADY
               
              BONA
text: When I have heard your king’s desert recounted,

speaker:
LADY
               
              BONA
text: Mine ear hath tempted judgment to desire.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Then, Warwick, thus: our sister shall be Edward’s.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: And now forthwith shall articles be drawn

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Touching the jointure that your king must make,

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Which with her dowry shall be counterpoised.—

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Draw near, Queen Margaret, and be a witness

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: That Bona shall be wife to the English king.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: To Edward, but not to the English king.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Deceitful Warwick, it was thy device

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: By this alliance to make void my suit.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Before thy coming, Lewis was Henry’s friend.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: And still is friend to him and Margaret.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: But if your title to the crown be weak,

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: As may appear by Edward’s good success,

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Then ’tis but reason that I be released

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: From giving aid which late I promisèd.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Yet shall you have all kindness at my hand

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: That your estate requires and mine can yield.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Henry now lives in Scotland at his ease,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Where, having nothing, nothing can he lose.—

speaker: WARWICK
text: And as for you yourself, our quondam queen,

speaker: WARWICK
text: You have a father able to maintain you,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And better ’twere you troubled him than France.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Peace, impudent and shameless Warwick,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Proud setter-up and puller-down of kings!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I will not hence till with my talk and tears,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Both full of truth, I make King Lewis behold

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thy sly conveyance and thy lord’s false love,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For both of you are birds of selfsame feather.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Warwick, this is some post to us or thee.

speaker: POST
text: My lord ambassador, these letters are for you,

speaker: POST
text: Sent from your brother, Marquess Montague.

speaker: POST
text:
(
                To
                 
                Lewis
                .
                )
                 
              These from our king unto your Majesty.

speaker: POST
text:
(
                To
                 
                Margaret
                .
                )
                 
              And, madam, these for you—from whom, I know not.

speaker: OXFORD
text: I like it well that our fair queen and mistress

speaker: OXFORD
text: Smiles at her news, while Warwick frowns at his.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: Nay, mark how Lewis stamps as he were nettled.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: I hope all’s for the best.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Warwick, what are thy news? And yours, fair queen?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Mine, such as fill my heart with unhoped joys.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Mine, full of sorrow and heart’s discontent.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: What, has your king married the Lady Grey,

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: And now, to soothe your forgery and his,

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Sends me a paper to persuade me patience?

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Is this th’ alliance that he seeks with France?

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Dare he presume to scorn us in this manner?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I told your Majesty as much before.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: This proveth Edward’s love and Warwick’s honesty.

speaker: WARWICK
text: King Lewis, I here protest in sight of heaven

speaker: WARWICK
text: And by the hope I have of heavenly bliss,

speaker: WARWICK
text: That I am clear from this misdeed of Edward’s—

speaker: WARWICK
text: No more my king, for he dishonors me,

speaker: WARWICK
text: But most himself, if he could see his shame.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Did I forget that by the house of York

speaker: WARWICK
text: My father came untimely to his death?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Did I let pass th’ abuse done to my niece?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Did I impale him with the regal crown?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Did I put Henry from his native right?

speaker: WARWICK
text: And am I guerdoned at the last with shame?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Shame on himself, for my desert is honor!

speaker: WARWICK
text: And to repair my honor lost for him,

speaker: WARWICK
text: I here renounce him and return to Henry.

speaker: WARWICK
text: My noble queen, let former grudges pass,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And henceforth I am thy true servitor.

speaker: WARWICK
text: I will revenge his wrong to Lady Bona

speaker: WARWICK
text: And replant Henry in his former state.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Warwick, these words have turned my hate to love,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And I forgive and quite forget old faults,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And joy that thou becom’st King Henry’s friend.

speaker: WARWICK
text: So much his friend, ay, his unfeignèd friend,

speaker: WARWICK
text: That if King Lewis vouchsafe to furnish us

speaker: WARWICK
text: With some few bands of chosen soldiers,

speaker: WARWICK
text: I’ll undertake to land them on our coast

speaker: WARWICK
text: And force the tyrant from his seat by war.

speaker: WARWICK
text: ’Tis not his new-made bride shall succor him.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And as for Clarence, as my letters tell me,

speaker: WARWICK
text: He’s very likely now to fall from him

speaker: WARWICK
text: For matching more for wanton lust than honor,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Or than for strength and safety of our country.

speaker:
LADY
               
              BONA
text: Dear brother, how shall Bona be revenged

speaker:
LADY
               
              BONA
text: But by thy help to this distressèd queen?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Renownèd prince, how shall poor Henry live

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Unless thou rescue him from foul despair?

speaker:
LADY
               
              BONA
text: My quarrel and this English queen’s are one.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And mine, fair Lady Bona, joins with yours.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: And mine with hers and thine and Margaret’s.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Therefore at last I firmly am resolved

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: You shall have aid.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Let me give humble thanks for all, at once.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Then, England’s messenger, return in post,

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: And tell false Edward, thy supposèd king,

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: That Lewis of France is sending over maskers

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: To revel it with him and his new bride.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Thou seest what’s passed; go fear thy king withal.

speaker:
LADY
               
              BONA
text: Tell him, in hope he’ll prove a widower shortly,

speaker:
LADY
               
              BONA
text: I wear the willow garland for his sake.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Tell him my mourning weeds are laid aside

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And I am ready to put armor on.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Tell him from me that he hath done me wrong,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And therefore I’ll uncrown him ere ’t be long.

speaker: WARWICK
text: There’s thy reward.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Be gone.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: But, Warwick,

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Thou and Oxford with five thousand men

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Shall cross the seas and bid false Edward battle;

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: And as occasion serves, this noble queen

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: And prince shall follow with a fresh supply.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Yet ere thou go, but answer me one doubt:

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: What pledge have we of thy firm loyalty?

speaker: WARWICK
text: This shall assure my constant loyalty:

speaker: WARWICK
text: That if our queen and this young prince agree,

speaker: WARWICK
text: I’ll join mine eldest daughter, and my joy,

speaker: WARWICK
text: To him forthwith in holy wedlock bands.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Yes, I agree, and thank you for your motion.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Son Edward, she is fair and virtuous.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Therefore, delay not; give thy hand to Warwick,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And with thy hand, thy faith irrevocable,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That only Warwick’s daughter shall be thine.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: Yes, I accept her, for she well deserves it,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: And here, to pledge my vow, I give my hand.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Why stay we now? These soldiers shall be levied,

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: And thou, Lord Bourbon, our High Admiral,

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: Shall waft them over with our royal fleet.

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: I long till Edward fall by war’s mischance

speaker:
KING
               
              LEWIS
text: For mocking marriage with a dame of France.

speaker: WARWICK
text: I came from Edward as ambassador,

speaker: WARWICK
text: But I return his sworn and mortal foe.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Matter of marriage was the charge he gave me,

speaker: WARWICK
text: But dreadful war shall answer his demand.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Had he none else to make a stale but me?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Then none but I shall turn his jest to sorrow.

speaker: WARWICK
text: I was the chief that raised him to the crown,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And I’ll be chief to bring him down again:

speaker: WARWICK
text: Not that I pity Henry’s misery,

speaker: WARWICK
text: But seek revenge on Edward’s mockery.
